review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the NIST AU-10(3) control, focusing on audit logging and RBAC to ensure non-repudiation and chain of custody. The naming convention mostly follows the pattern, but a slight adjustment was made for clarity and specificity in the RBAC function.
naming_improvements: Ensure the function name clearly indicates the specific aspect of audit log access control being configured.
title: Non-Repudiation - Non - repudiation | chain of custody maintain reviewer or releaser credentials w
step1_reasoning: The AU-10(3) control focuses on non-repudiation and maintaining a chain of custody for reviewer or releaser credentials. In Kubernetes, this can be addressed by ensuring audit logging is enabled to capture access and changes to resources, which supports non-repudiation by providing a traceable log of actions. Additionally, configuring audit log retention ensures that logs are preserved for a sufficient period to support investigations. RBAC controls can be configured to ensure that only authorized users have access to audit logs, maintaining the integrity of the chain of custody.
